Verdict: A 10/10 stop for nature lovers and photographers alike. It was one of the highlights of our road trip!

I had this on our itinerary for our San Jose visit, and I was blown away by how beautiful the space is. The garden is perfectly organized with dozens of flower beds, each showcasing a single variety of rose. In the center sits a peaceful water fountain, and the overall atmosphere is exceptionally serene.

The Experience:

Dining & Relaxing: One side of the garden has large, open grass areas that are ideal for relaxing or enjoying an alfresco lunch (like locals do!).

Photography: If you are visiting in April, it is the absolute perfect time of year for family photos. The backdrop of layered colors—from the bright yellows to the deep pinks—is world-class.

The Arbor: Don't miss the large climbing rose arbors; they offer a bit of shade and a stunning tunnel-like effect for pictures.

Important Notes:

Pet Policy: Please note that pets are not allowed inside the garden to protect the delicate rose beds.

Accessibility: Most of the paths here are flat and easy to navigate, making it a very accessible stop compared to other hilly gardens in the area.

It's only a 5-minute drive from the shopping at Valley Fair and Santana Row, making it a great peaceful break between stops.  

Check out my reviews of other spots in San Jose here:  https://www.tripadvisor.com/members-citypage/TravelsNTreasures/g33020.

We enjoyed this large green area, voted America's best rose garden.  We played, here, enjoyed a picnic on a blanket on the grass, explored all the over 200 varieties of roses and over 3500 shrubs. 
People enjoy picnics, volleyball with bring your own net, and the beautiful fountain that is closed off by a wrought iron fence.  The greenery is set out with sidewalks with wide smooth accessible surface.  Many people come her for wedding, prom, quinceañello photos.  The fountain is relaxing esp with a few benches for sitting.  Note that the large trees provide shade and there are many full sun areas.
The roses are in beds with name markers and fill out the wrought iron fences that surround the park.  Note that the gates are closed one hour after sunset and open at 8am. I also found a yoga program.  
Our grand child made new friends here to play with. 
The mens restroom is on one side and womens on the other with dedicated stall with 2 grab bars.  pay phone by mens room

No pets in the garden although many bring their to walk the perimeter on the outside with a pet waste station.  no playground.

Unfortunatelyh, we've visitedi n the wintertime because that's when we went to San Jose. However, I think it's much better and more colorful in the summertime .Not a bad experience though. Allow an hour or two. Well priced, friendly staff, best for couplesand families
